news.Title: Broadcom Just Hit $2 Trillion Market Cap. Is AVGO Stock a Buy Now?
news.Content:
Broadcom’s (AVGO) recent march to a $2 trillion valuation was no accident. It happened due to the market’s renewed interest in artificial intelligence (AI) and a series of catalysts that strengthened its key role in next-generation computing. On April 22, Broadcom briefly crossed the $2 trillion market capitalization mark after AVGO stock rallied to record highs, including a move near $422. The trigger wasn’t just its deepening partnership with Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google, but a powerful mix of AI-fueled earnings growth and blockbuster multi-year deals with Anthropic and other AI leaders.

Together, these forces pushed Broadcom into the exclusive $2 trillion club, signaling that the market now views the firm as more than just a semiconductor company. While Broadcom shares have surged 16% year-to-date (YTD), outperforming the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite ($NASX), the stock has also rallied 32% in just the last month alone.

More News from Barchart

Why the Real Story Behind the UAE’s OPEC Exit is Petrodollar Diplomacy

Should You Buy X-Energy Stock After the Amazon-Backed XE IPO?

Massively Disappointing Boston Scientific (BSX) Stock Could Be Due for a Comeback

Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines.

Is AVGO stock a good buy now?

Why Broadcom’s Strategic Partnerships Matter

Broadcom and Google’s partnership is becoming deeper than just a typical supplier relationship. Recently, Google announced its move to split its latest Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) into two specialized chips, with one aimed at training and the other targeting inference. This move dramatically increases both the complexity and the scale of chip demand — and, being Google’s supplier for these specialized chips, it directly benefits Broadcom. The company's agreement with Google extends through 2031, effectively landing itself deep within Google’s AI infrastructure roadmap and making it difficult to replace.

At the same time, Broadcom's collaboration with Anthropic is expected to unlock roughly 3.5 gigawatts (GW) of compute capacity starting in 2027, signaling massive scaling potential. These strategic deals are multi-year commitments that provide visibility, reduce customer churn risk, and lock in recurring AI-driven revenue streams.

Explosive Growth Backed by AI Demand

Broadcom’s first-quarter report is also one of the reasons the market rewarded AVGO stock so aggressively. Revenue surged 29% year-over-year (YOY) to $19.31 billion. Semiconductor revenue reached $12.5 billion, up 52% YOY, accounting for 65% of total revenue. Notably, AI semiconductor revenue exploded by 106% to $8.4 billion from the prior-year quarter.

Story Continues

What’s even more impressive is that Broadcom expects semiconductor revenue to reach $14.8 billion in Q2, marking 76% YOY growth. Within that, AI semiconductor revenue is projected at $10.7 billion, representing staggering 140% growth. This growth is the result of its custom AI accelerators (XPUs) deployed across major customers, including hyperscalers and AI leaders like Google, Meta Platforms (META), and OpenAI. Broadcom revealed it is now working with six major AI customers, with multi-year, multi-gigawatt deployment plans already in place.

Broadcom plans to generate more than $100 billion in AI chip revenue by 2027. This single number also motivated investors to push the stock to new highs. Beyond chips, Broadcom is capitalizing on AI networking, with networking revenue up 60% YOY in Q1. Broadcom generated free cash flow of $8 billion during the quarter, accounting for 41% of revenue, and returned $10.9 billion to shareholders in a single quarter through dividends and share buybacks. The company’s adjusted EPS increased 28% YOY in Q1, and analysts predict EPS growth of 77% for the full fiscal year, followed by 64% growth in fiscal 2027.

Is AVGO Stock a Buy Now?

Broadcom’s fundamentals are undeniably strong. Very few semiconductor companies are delivering this level of hypergrowth while maintaining strong margins and massive free cash flow. The company's position at the center of the AI infrastructure boom, combined with deep partnerships with AI leaders, has helped it earn its trillion-dollar valuation. Broadcom is no longer a hidden long-term AI opportunity, but it remains a name that investors should strongly consider.

Wall Street cheered Broadcom’s game-changing deals with Google and Anthropic. Analysts have a consensus “Strong Buy" rating on AVGO stock. Of the 42 analysts with coverage, 35 analysts have a “Strong Buy,” three have a “Moderate Buy” rating, and four recommend a “Hold.” Based on the average target price of $469.94, the stock has potential upside of 18% from current levels. Plus, the high price target of $630 suggests shares could climb by 58% over the next 12 months.

news.Title: Micron Stock Just Got a New Street-High Price Target. Should You Buy Shares Now?
news.Content:
Micron Technology (MU) is a global leader in innovative memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of data into intelligence. As a vertically integrated semiconductor giant, Micron specializes in DRAM and NAND technologies, providing the essential foundation for data centers, mobile devices, and automotive systems. In 2026, the company has solidified its position as a strategic enabler of the AI revolution, specifically through its leadership in High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM).

Founded in 1978, Micron is headquartered in Boise, Idaho.

More News from Barchart

Why the Real Story Behind the UAE’s OPEC Exit is Petrodollar Diplomacy

Should You Buy X-Energy Stock After the Amazon-Backed XE IPO?

Massively Disappointing Boston Scientific (BSX) Stock Could Be Due for a Comeback

Get exclusive insights with the FREE Barchart Brief newsletter. Subscribe now for quick, incisive midday market analysis you won't find anywhere else.

Micron Stock Rallies

Micron's stock has experienced a historic "Supercycle" rally, surging over 560% in the last year to reach all-time highs above $530. This explosive growth is fueled by a global memory shortage and soaring demand for AI-grade memory chips.

Compared with the Nasdaq Composite ($NASX), Micron has delivered staggering alpha over the trailing 12 months. While the tech-heavy Nasdaq grew by a robust 41%, Micron’s 560% return outperformed the broader market by nearly 14 times. MU is significantly more volatile than the index, reflecting its high sensitivity to semiconductor cycles and AI capital expenditure trends.

Micron Reports Record Numbers

Micron delivered a "record-shattering" performance for its fiscal second quarter of 2026, reported on March 18, 2026. The company posted revenue of $23.86 billion, an astounding 196% increase year-over-year (YoY), significantly beating the analyst consensus of $19.19 billion. Non-GAAP EPS reached $12.20, a 682% surge from the prior year, driven by record gross margins of 75%.

This performance was fueled by a "Golden Era" in memory demand, particularly in the Cloud Memory and Mobile segments. The company ended the quarter with $16.7 billion in cash and approved a 30% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.15 per share.

Micron’s guidance for Q3 2026 is even more aggressive, with projected revenue of $33.5 billion and an EPS of $19.15. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra noted that memory has become a "defining strategic asset" in the AI era, with HBM capacity already sold out through the remainder of the calendar year. Management's outlook is bolstered by the ramp-up of HBM4 production and a new five-year Strategic Customer Agreement, providing unprecedented long-term visibility.

Story Continues

Micron Receives Street-High Rating

MU stock dipped 3.8% yesterday despite a landmark "Buy" initiation from DA Davidson with a Wall Street-high price target of $1,000, reflecting an upside of 98% from the market rate. Analyst Gil Luria argues that artificial intelligence has triggered a "positive feedback loop" in memory demand, where each compute deployment creates new use cases and incremental demand, structurally raising the ceiling for memory pricing. Unlike previous cycles defined by oversupply, the current landscape is stabilized by five-year Strategic Customer Agreements (SCAs) that provide unprecedented visibility and lock in demand.

Luria highlights Micron’s node leadership across DRAM, HBM, and NAND as a critical competitive advantage that improves cost positions and market share in high-margin products. While the broader market remains cautious, viewing the stock through the lens of past downturns, DA Davidson suggests the industry is in a "Golden Era" of earnings power.

Looking ahead to fiscal 2030, the firm projects Micron could reach $393 billion in revenue and $139 in adjusted earnings per share, implying that the current memory supercycle is significantly underappreciated by investors.

Should You Buy MU Stock?

Despite recent volatility, Micron remains a top conviction play for the AI supercycle. While DA Davidson’s $1,000 price target represents the high-water mark for optimism, the broader market maintains a consensus "Strong Buy" rating. The mean price target of $546.09 suggests a modest 7% upside from current levels. Professional sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, with 31 "Strong Buy" ratings, five "Moderate Sells," and five "Holds."

Analysts believe Micron’s node leadership and locked-in long-term demand via strategic agreements make it an essential holding as memory becomes a primary bottleneck in AI scaling.

This widely-known consensus outlook is important in assessing the company's earnings picture, but a powerful factor that might influence its near-term stock price is how the actual results compare to these estimates. The stock might move higher if these key numbers top expectations in the upcoming earnings report, which is expected to be released on May 5. On the other hand, if they miss, the stock may ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended March 2026.
• The consensus EPS estimate is $1.30, representing a 35.4% year-over-year increase, with revenues expected at $9.84 billion, up 32.3%.
• The consensus EPS estimate has been revised 1.84% higher over the last 30 days, indicating a bullish sentiment among analysts.
• AMD has a positive Earnings ESP of +5.02% and a Zacks Rank of #2, suggesting a high likelihood of beating the consensus EPS estimate.
news.Keywords: earnings growth, revenue expectations, EPS surprise, Zacks Rank, estimate revisions

news.Content: The "Magnificent Seven" moniker was coined by Wall Street in 2023 to describe a group of seven powerhouse technology companies. They are known for consistently producing better returns than the rest of the market thanks to their dominance in industries like consumer hardware, enterprise software, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence (AI). But the Magnificent Seven stocks are off to a rough start to 2026, with just three of them outperforming the S&P 500 index ...

• DigitalOcean competes with Microsoft, Alphabet, and Amazon in cloud computing, offering AI tools and services that are supercharging its revenue growth.
• The company targets startups and SMBs with affordable pricing and personalized service, making AI deployment easier.

news.AI_Summary:

• The bond offering spans maturities from five to 40 years, with early pricing indicating the longest-dated bonds could come at a spread of about 1.65 percentage points above Treasuries.
• This financing effort follows Intel's announcement to take on roughly $6.5 billion in new debt to repurchase a 49% stake in the Irish joint venture.

news.Keywords: Intel, bond sale, Ireland plant, manufacturing strategy, AI demand

news.Title: Microsoft vs. Amazon: Which AI Stock Is the Better Buy?
news.Content: Not every artificial intelligence (AI) stock is a long-term winner. During the past five years, Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) and Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) have both underperformed the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) . Amazon gained 52% and Microsoft rose 68%, while the S&P 500 increased 71% during that timeframe. And if you look at more recent performance, these two AI stocks are heading in different directions. Microsoft shares have declined about 12% year to date, while Amazon has gained 14%. Amazon has also outperformed other leading AI stocks like Nvidia and Alphabet so far this year. Will AI create the world's first ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon is rated a better buy than Microsoft, with its AI capital expenditures potentially paying off, evidenced by AWS AI services achieving a $15 billion annual revenue run rate.
• Amazon's AI investments are seen as having a clearer path to monetization compared to other companies making less profitable bets.
• Microsoft is perceived as a laggard in the AI race, with its Copilot AI tools struggling to gain traction, as only 3% of commercial Office customers have bought licenses.
• Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI is becoming complicated, and competitors like Google Gemini and Anthropic Claude pose challenges.
news.Keywords: Amazon, Microsoft, AI investment, AWS, Copilot, competition

news.Title: 3 Green Energy Stocks for Investors Playing the Long Game in 2026
news.Content: There's no denying the green energy mania that enthralled investors in the industry's not-so-recent past has since cooled. Nuclear power seems to be all the rage now, and fossil fuels aren't fading away nearly as quickly as previously anticipated. If you think renewables stocks don't offer investors enough upside to bother with them, however, think again. The United States' Energy Information Administration reports that a little over half of the nation's installed power capacity in 2025 was solar, with wind accounting for another 14%. Natural gas and coal still collectively produce the majority of the country's electricity, but coal's share ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Renewable energy market is expected to grow at an average annual pace of nearly 14% through 2031.
• Bloom Energy's revenue grew 37% last year to over $2 billion, with an operating profit of nearly $73 million.
• Cameco, a uranium miner, generated nearly $3.5 billion in revenue last year.
• The article highlights Bloom Energy's solid oxide fuel cells and Cameco's position in the uranium market as long-term investment opportunities.
news.Keywords: green energy, renewable energy, Bloom Energy, Cameco, uranium

news.Title: Intel Just Shocked Everyone, but Is the Stock Still a Buy?
news.Content: Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) shocked the market when its quarterly results zoomed past analyst expectations. The stock has now approximately doubled in less than a month. While analysts praised the quarter and Intel's CEO after the results, it actually looks much more like a case of the company stumbling into a hot ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel's Q1 results surpassed expectations, with revenue rising 7% to $13.6 billion and adjusted EPS doubling to $0.29, driven by surging demand for data center CPUs for AI agents.
• The DCAI product revenue increased 22% to $5.1 billion, and the foundry business saw revenue climb 16% to $5.4 billion, though it reported a $2.4 billion operating loss.
• Gross margin improved to 39.4%, and Intel projects Q2 revenue between $13.8-$14.8 billion with adjusted EPS of $0.20, exceeding analyst expectations.
• Despite a strong quarter, the article suggests Intel stumbled into the AI trend and prefers AMD for playing the data center CPU trend due to Intel's foundry issues and high valuation.
news.Keywords: Intel, Q1 results, AI demand, data center CPUs, foundry business

news.Title: Should You Rush to Get in on the SpaceX IPO? History Offers a Strikingly Clear Answer.
news.Content: Investors are always on the lookout for the stock market's next big growth story. This could happen with a stock that's already been on the market for a long while -- artificial intelligence (AI) giant Nvidia is a great example. news.AI_Summary:

• SpaceX's businesses include rocket launches, satellite internet service Starlink, and the AI business xAI.
• History suggests that investors do not need to rush into IPOs, as many companies offer multiple entry points after their debut.

news.Title: Analysis-SpaceX ties Musk compensation to Mars colonization goal
news.Content: By Ross Kerber April 28 (Reuters) - SpaceX's board has approved a compensation plan for founder Elon Musk with goals as futuristic and celestial as the company's ambitions: colonizing Mars and running data centers in outer space. The details of Musk's sweeping pay package, which have not been widely reported, were revealed in the ‌company's confidential registration statement filed in recent weeks with the Securities and Exchange Commission and reviewed by Reuters last week. More from Yahoo Scout How does ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's board approved a compensation plan for Elon Musk tied to futuristic goals like colonizing Mars and running data centers in outer space.
• Musk could receive 200 million restricted shares if SpaceX hits a $7.5 trillion market value and establishes a Mars colony, or up to 60.4 million shares for operating space data centers.
• These awards come with super-voting Class B restricted stock and vest in tranches based on company value.
• SpaceX is targeting an IPO around June 28, potentially valuing the company at $1.75 trillion, and Musk's compensation structure could create tensions with Tesla shareholders.
news.Keywords: Elon Musk compensation, SpaceX, Mars colonization, space data centers, IPO valuation

news.Title: OpenAI trial pitting Elon Musk against Sam Altman kicks off
news.Content: By Deepa Seetharaman OAKLAND, California, April 28 (Reuters) - A trial that could help shape the future of artificial intelligence begins on Tuesday, with billionaires Elon Musk and Sam Altman at odds over the evolution of ChatGPT maker OpenAI from a nonprofit to a ‌profit-seeking juggernaut worth hundreds of billions of dollars. More from Yahoo Scout How could this trial impact OpenAI's IPO plans? ...
news.AI_Summary:
• A trial between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over OpenAI's evolution from nonprofit to profit-seeking entity begins, potentially shaping the future of AI.
• Musk alleges Altman and Greg Brockman betrayed OpenAI's mission and turned it into a 'wealth machine,' seeking $150 billion in damages from OpenAI and Microsoft.
• Musk wants OpenAI to revert to a nonprofit with Altman and Brockman removed as officers.
• OpenAI counters that Musk knew about and supported the transformation, suing only after failing to become CEO and starting his own AI company, xAI.
news.Keywords: Elon Musk, Sam Altman, OpenAI, AI trial, nonprofit vs profit

news.Title: Big Tech investors to gauge payoff as AI spending set to hit $600 billion

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Australia to charge Big Tech companies 2% levy unless they strike local news deals
news.Content: By Christine Chen and Byron Kaye SYDNEY, April 28 (Reuters) - The Australian government said on Tuesday that Meta, Alphabet's Google and TikTok could face multimillion-dollar ‌charges if they did not negotiate deals to pay local media outlets ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Australia is proposing a 2.25% levy on local revenues for Meta, Google, and TikTok if they fail to strike deals with local media outlets for news content.
• The 'News Bargaining Incentive' aims to support Australian journalism by channeling funds to news organizations based on journalist employment.
• The government argues that digital platforms benefiting from news content should contribute financially to its creation.
• This initiative could face opposition from the U.S. government, which opposes digital services taxes on its tech giants.
• The levy is intended to replace previous, less effective regulations mandating payments for news content.
news.Keywords: digital services tax, media payments, Australian journalism, regulatory levy, platform revenue

news.Title: Poet Technologies Stock Crashed After Marvell Canceled Orders: What Comes Next?

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Meta pokes holes in China's great AI firewall
news.Content: (The author is a Reuters Breakingviews columnist. The opinions expressed are her own.) By Karen Kwok NEW YORK, April 27 (Reuters Breakingviews) - Meta Platforms has exposed a recurring dilemma for China: the country wants its technology champions to ‌go global, but not to leave home. Beijing effectively ordered the social-media goliath to unwind its $2 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• China has ordered Meta Platforms to unwind its $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns and tightening oversight of U.S. investment in frontier technologies.
• The order comes after Manus had largely extricated itself from China, shifting operations to Singapore and integrating into Meta's AI operations.
• Reversing the transaction will be tricky, with questions remaining about recovering funds from earlier backers and separating integrated people, code, and intellectual property.
• Beijing's move could reflect a broader policy shift towards tighter oversight of AI sector capital flows and technology transfer.
• The timing, weeks before a U.S.-China summit, suggests the decision may be linked to intensifying strategic competition over AI capabilities.
news.Keywords: acquisition block, national security, AI technology transfer, regulatory oversight, strategic competition

news.Title: China AI Models Narrow Gap, Undercut US Pricing by Up to 33x
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Despite limited access to the most advanced US-designed chips and comparatively smaller ...
news.AI_Summary:
• China's AI companies like Alibaba and DeepSeek are developing models that perform comparably to top U.S. systems but use significantly less computing power, leading to much lower pricing.
• DeepSeek's V4-Pro model uses less than 3% of its parameters, resulting in pricing as low as $0.42 per million output tokens compared to OpenAI's GPT-5.2 at $14.
• Chinese developers favor open-weight systems, accelerating adoption, with Alibaba's Qwen models surpassing 1 billion downloads.
• State support, including electricity subsidies and AI initiatives, plays a significant role in China's AI deployment.
• The efficiency-driven approach and lower costs of Chinese AI platforms could pressure premium pricing strategies of U.S. AI leaders.
news.Keywords: AI cost efficiency, open-weight models, state support, pricing pressure, global market share

news.Title: Elon Musk's trial against Sam Altman to reveal the ongoing power struggle for OpenAI

news.Title: Concerned About Social Security Insolvency? Here's What's Likely to Happen Next
news.Content: For millions of retirees, Social Security is the bedrock of financial security. Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Social Security trust funds are projected to run dry by 2033, meaning the program will rely on current payroll taxes for about 75%-80% of scheduled benefits.
• Possible legislative actions include increasing the payroll tax, raising the full retirement age, or removing the payroll tax cap.
• A combination of smaller policy adjustments is considered the most likely approach to ensure the program's solvency.
• Current retirees and those nearing retirement are unlikely to face benefit cuts due to political sensitivity.

news.Title: Cadence Expands TSMC Alliance to Boost Advanced AI Chip Design
news.Content: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. CDNS has deepened its long-standing collaboration with TSMC to accelerate innovation in AI-driven semiconductor design, reinforcing its leadership in enabling next-generation silicon. This expanded partnership focuses on delivering silicon-proven IP, signoff-ready end-to-end design infrastructure and certified flows for advanced AI chips built on TSMC’s most cutting-edge process technologies, including N3, N2, A16 and A14. • Cadence is delivering a comprehensive IP portfolio optimized for TSMC nodes, including HBM4E, and its Artisan foundation IP is deployed in production designs on TSMC's 3nm technologies.
• The integration of agentic AI across Cadence's platforms, like Genus Synthesis and Innovus Implementation, enhances optimization for TSMC's advanced architectures.

news.Content: Though Tesla ( TSLA ) stock is lower following the release of its first quarter earnings report , Wall Street analysts are generally encouraged, though cautious. news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's Q1 earnings showed a $25 billion+ capital expenditure plan for 2026, a significant increase from previous forecasts, to accelerate AI, robotics, and expansion.
• The company expects negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026 due to these investments, despite a $1.44 billion Q1 surplus.
• Analysts expressed caution, noting that the capex increase highlights Tesla's ambitious scale but implies strongly negative free cash flow.
• While Tesla's AI investments are 'bearing fruit' with FSD subscriptions up, the robotaxi rollout is 'slow and steady,' with meaningful revenue not expected until 2027.

news.Title: Why the Fed's Next Move Could Be the Most Important Catalyst for Stocks This Spring
news.Content: Oil prices have surged over the past two months, as world leaders fail to resolve the war in Iran. The impasse has resulted in higher gas prices for consumers, with the national average at $4.02 per gallon, according to AAA. It's also led to higher overall inflation. Plastic products, fertilizers, and other items that use oil in the manufacturing process are now more expensive to produce, and pricier fuel drives up shipping costs. Will AI create the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Oil prices have surged due to the war in Iran, leading to higher inflation (3.3% YoY) and increased pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
• Fed Chair Jerome Powell is taking a 'wait and see' approach but acknowledges the tension between controlling inflation and supporting economic growth.
• Raising interest rates could slow economic growth and increase unemployment.
• Typically, lower interest rates benefit the stock market, but sustained inflation could force the Fed to raise rates, potentially causing a stock market drop.
• Investors are advised to maintain a long-term outlook amidst market uncertainty.
news.Keywords: oil prices, inflation, Federal Reserve, interest rates, economic growth, unemployment, stock market
